summary: Iteration 5 (Transactions tab refactor: grouped rows + receipt dialog + CSV download) verified end-to-end. Backend pytest regression: 34/34 PASS (no backend changes; auth/users/products/transactions/bulk all green). Frontend: tx-list now renders ONE row per cart (group key = type|user_id|created_at|note). Verified against live API: 44 raw line items collapse into 35 UI rows; UI rows count exactly matches the deduplicated group set computed from /api/transactions. Filters: All=35, Buy=21 (only BUY chips), Sell=14 (only SELL chips), reset to 35. tx-search matches product, user email AND note independently (verified note search snippet 'TEST_bulk_' returns 9 rows, identical to API-computed expectation). Empty state (tx-empty) renders for no-match query. Each row exposes tx-row-{groupId} (e.g. tx-row-6a1fdbd99f99277be6e27934), tx-row-total-{id} (₱-formatted), short reference '#E6E27934' (last-8 uppercase), headline 'TEST_Bulk_a_32df + 1 more' for multi-item carts, single product name for one-line carts, plus '<N> item · <M> unit(s)'. Clicking row opens tx-details-dialog populated with tx-details-user, tx-details-date (long format), N tx-details-item-{id} entries (verified biggest group has 4 children -> dialog shows 4 items), tx-details-total in ₱, and the dialog closes via tx-details-close-btn (count=0 in DOM after click). tx-details-download-btn triggers a real browser download captured via page.expect_download(); filename matches the spec pattern '${type}-${YYYYMMDDHHMM}-${idTail6}.csv' (captured: 'buy-202606030746-e27934.csv', 269 bytes, valid CSV header 'Stockroom Purchase Receipt' + reference + product rows + totals). tx-stat-count shows '35' with subtitle '44 line item(s)' confirming grouped-vs-raw distinction.
critical_code_review_comments: Grouping key in TransactionsTab.jsx (`${type}|${user_id}|${created_at}|${note||''}`) correctly identifies bulk-cart submissions because the backend stamps every line of a bulk POST with the same created_at + actor + note. Verified against real data (44 line items -> 35 stable groups).

Group id derivation = first child's id (`g.id.slice(-8).toUpperCase()` for #ref, `slice(-6)` for CSV filename suffix) is deterministic per server response so the data-testid `tx-row-{id}` is stable across refreshes — good for automation.
Search predicate correctly OR's (product_name | user_email | note) — confirmed parity with manually computed expectation count.

CSV builder properly CSV-escapes product names containing quotes (`String(it.product_name).replace(/"/g, '""')`) and the note field. Numeric fields use .toFixed(2). Filename uses ISO date stripped of separators.
Memory hygiene: URL.createObjectURL is paired with URL.revokeObjectURL after click — no blob leak.
Dialog guards on `if (!group) return null` so closing animation can't crash on null group. onOpenChange wired correctly in parent to reset detailsGroup.
Stat card subtitle ('44 line item(s)') keeps the raw count visible for audit reconciliation — nice UX touch.

Minor (non-blocking): groupTransactions runs on every render of items list through useMemo (good), but localeCompare per group also runs on every memo recompute; fine at this scale (<100 items) — flag only if scale grows.

Minor (non-blocking): the empty-state copy says 'No transactions yet' even when the cause is a non-matching search — consider conditional text ('No matches for "<query>"') for clarity. Not a bug; UX polish only.

success_rate.frontend: 100% of validated flows (all 9 spec items confirmed)

test_credentials: Admin: admin@example.com / admin123 (works, used for all UI checks).
seed_data_creation: No new seed data. Used existing seeded transactions (44 line items across 35 carts including multi-item bulk submissions and single-line txs, with both 'TEST_bulk_buy' and 'sari-sari order' notes present — exercised search-by-note path).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 5 was frontend-only and is fully green. Grouping logic key = type|user_id|created_at|note (TransactionsTab.jsx). CSV download fires through page.expect_download(); filename pattern ${type}-${YYYYMMDDHHMM}-${idTail6}.csv. All required data-testids confirmed live: tx-stat-count, tx-search, tx-filter-all/buy/sell, tx-list, tx-row-{id}, tx-row-total-{id}, tx-empty, tx-details-dialog, tx-details-user, tx-details-date, tx-details-note (only when present), tx-details-item-{id}, tx-details-total, tx-details-close-btn, tx-details-download-btn. Backend untouched since iter-4; pytest still 34/34. No regressions in iter-4 features.
